metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verify command and AC to task 43c9 that confirms llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md was updated (e.g., `grep -n 'test-design\|quantified\|multiple samples' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md` hits in the Phase 2 / RED-artifact section), and add the file to file_impact.
findings.finding: Task 43c9 instructs updating llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md where it has single-eval RED-artifact wording, but no AC and no verify_command covers this update — it is a processing rule with no placement or verification anchor.
findings.location: Task 43c9 description — 'llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md is checked for any single-eval RED-artifact wording and, where present, gains a pointer to the shared standard's quantified-eval requirements so the two files cannot disagree'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item and file_impact entry for llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md in child 43c9, with a prove command (e.g. `grep -n 'test-design.md' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md` hits in the relevant paragraph).
findings.finding: Child 43c9's What section explicitly names updating llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md as a deliverable (pointer to shared standard's quantified-eval requirements) but this file is absent from file_impact and has no corresponding AC verification item — a partial-without-remainder: the deliverable is described but unchecked.
findings.location: child 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 — What section (rebar-debug integration paragraph); Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 43c9 file_impact: `{"path": "examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md", "reason": "add pointer to shared eval standard"}`. Add AC item: 'llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md gains a pointer to the shared standard's quantified-eval requirements where single-eval wording was present. Prove: `grep -n "test-design.md" examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md` returns at least one hit.'

findings.finding: Child 43c9's rebar-implement integration has no content-level acceptance criterion — only a grep for the presence of the reference string. The What section specifies substantive integration ('Phase 4a/4b reference the shared file for oracle design (inventory, tier, oracle completeness, gating proof)'), but the ACs only verify that the string 'shared/test-design.md' appears somewhere in the file, not that it appears at the Phase 4a/4b oracle-design point with the specified substantive guidance. This is a scope-narrowing risk under the three-part coverage standard.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'In rebar-implement/SKILL.md, the shared/test-design.md reference appears within the Phase 4a or 4b section (oracle/test-design guidance), not solely in a footnote or notes section — verified by grep or inspection of the integration point.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verify command (or AC item) to child e8d3 asserting `git diff --exit-code -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E5.md` returns clean.
findings.finding: Parent AC item 'git diff shows no edit to plan_review_E5.md' is owned by neither child: child e8d3 guards criteria_routing.json and posture/threshold fields but does not mention plan_review_E5.md; child 43c9's scope is entirely in examples/claude-skills/ and tests/unit/ and has no guard on reviewers/.
findings.location: child e8d3-03b6-cf2d-46ae — Acceptance Criteria (no E5 guard); child 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 — Acceptance Criteria (no E5 guard)
findings.suggested_fix: In child e8d3's AC, extend the no-change assertion to: `git diff shows no change to plan_review_E5.md, criteria_routing.json (either gate), or any threshold/posture field.` Add a corresponding verify command: `git diff --exit-code -- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_E5.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the bug-class vocabulary (the left column of the bug-class→tier table) in the test-design.md spec — enumerate at least the bug classes that map to the listed tiers so the table is fully specified for executors.
findings.finding: The plan defines a 'bug-class → tier table' in test-design.md (task 43c9) and enumerates the tier column values, but never defines the bug-class vocabulary (the left-column enum). An executor writing the table must invent the bug-class taxonomy without a defined value set.
findings.location: Task 43c9 description — 'test-tier selection rule: the smallest tier that still crosses every component in the proven mechanism, with the bug-class -> tier table'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet enumerating the bug-class column values alongside the tier column, e.g.: 'pure-logic defect → unit; producer/consumer boundary → producer-consumer contract; CLI entry-point behavior → real CLI subprocess; git-repo state → real git repos; shared-memory race → multi-process with deterministic barrier; subprocess lifecycle → parent-child subprocess; crash/restart recovery → kill-and-restart crash recovery; stateful side-effect → stateful verified fake; prompt/instruction behavior → behavioral eval'.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the discovery strategy for `test_skill_shared_sync.py`: either enumerate skill dirs by scanning `examples/claude-skills/` for subdirectories containing `SKILL.md` (auto-discovers future skills) or document that the hardcoded list must be updated when a skill is added (and add that requirement to the sync one-liner note in the README).
findings.finding: The drift-gate test `tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py` is specified to 'FAIL when a copy diverges or is missing', but the plan does not specify how the test discovers which skill directories to check — a hardcoded list vs. an auto-discovery scan. If hardcoded to the current three skills, a future fourth skill shipping without a copy produces no gate failure, silently undermining the 'every copy' invariant the plan asserts. If auto-discovered by scanning for directories containing `test-design.md`, a skill that never received its copy is also invisible.
findings.location: Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 description — 'Reference mechanism' section; AC: `tests/unit/test_skill_shared_sync.py` assertion
findings.suggested_fix: In the test's specification, state that it scans `examples/claude-skills/` for any subdirectory containing a `SKILL.md` (i.e., a recognized skill) and asserts that each such directory also has a `test-design.md` byte-identical to the canonical copy. This auto-discovers future skills without requiring the test to be updated each time one is added.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the `diff` field content for FP-strict-xfail-with-reason and FP-sleep-as-secondary in task e8d3 (provide sample unified diffs showing, respectively, a `@pytest.mark.xfail(strict=True, reason='...')` decorated test and a race test using a barrier plus a short sleep), and add a verify step asserting both new cases carry a non-empty `diff`.
findings.finding: The two new no-fire eval cases (FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary) in task e8d3 are described scenarially but the plan never specifies their `diff` field content — yet test_code_review_dataset_balanced (an existing gating unit test) asserts `all(c.get('diff') for c in ds)` and will fail if either case lacks a diff payload.
findings.location: Task e8d3 description — 'no-fire FP-strict-xfail-with-reason (mode: strict-xfail-valid): a strict xfail with a reason naming a removal condition' and 'no-fire FP-sleep-as-secondary (mode: sleep-secondary-valid): a race test synchronized by a barrier/marker file with a short settling sleep'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the task e8d3 AC: `python -c "import yaml; ds = yaml.safe_load(open('src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml'))['dataset']; fps = [c for c in ds if c['id'] in ('FP-strict-xfail-with-reason','FP-sleep-as-secondary')]; assert all(c.get('diff') for c in fps), 'FP cases missing diff'`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `{"path": "src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml", "reason": "add four new eval cases (2 recall + 2 no-fire)"}` to child e8d3's file_impact.
findings.finding: Child e8d3's file_impact lists only code-review-tests.md but the What and AC also require changes to code-review-tests.eval.yaml — the eval YAML is missing from file_impact, creating a residual-reference gap where the completion verifier's file-impact manifest does not cover one of the two modified files.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to child e8d3 file_impact: `{"path": "src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-tests.eval.yaml", "reason": "add R-non-gating-skip, R-fault-wrong-seam, FP-strict-xfail-with-reason, FP-sleep-as-secondary eval cases"}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the expected integration mechanism for the shared test-design.md (e.g., 'added as a `<file>` or `<context>` include directive in each SKILL.md system prompt block') so the executor does not have to infer or choose.
findings.finding: 'Loaded by all three skills' in the Success Criteria does not specify the integration mechanism, leaving an executor to choose between a hyperlink/reference, a file-include directive, or inline copy — each produces meaningfully different skill behavior.
findings.location: Success Criteria — first bullet: 'One shared test-design reference exists under examples/claude-skills/shared/ and is loaded by all three skills at the point each incorporates testing.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Success Criteria or Task 43c9 description: 'Integration means the shared file is referenced via [mechanism used by existing skills, e.g., an explicit `<file src="../shared/test-design.md">` block] in each SKILL.md, not merely a hyperlink.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the minimum number of recall and no-fire eval cases required per antipattern (e.g., 'at least one recall case and one no-fire case each') so the criterion is unambiguously evaluable.
findings.finding: The Success Criterion 'each new antipattern has eval recall + no-fire coverage' does not specify a minimum number of cases, leaving the executor to decide whether one recall + one no-fire case each satisfies the criterion.
findings.location: Success Criteria — third bullet: '…each new antipattern has eval recall + no-fire cases for both…'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the third Success Criterion bullet: '(minimum: one recall case and one no-fire case per antipattern, matching the convention used by existing antipatterns in code-review-tests.eval.yaml).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a verifiable condition to AC1 (or a new AC) that explicitly confirms the examples README references or links the new shared test-design document.
findings.finding: The examples README update is described as a deliverable in the Decomposition ('integrate it into the three skills + the examples README') but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that the README was actually updated. This is an uncovered work item.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 to read: '…is Verified +1 and merged (task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900 closed), AND git diff of that change shows at least one added reference to shared/test-design.md in examples/claude-skills/README.md (or equivalent top-level README).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan (Context or Decomposition) what 'integration' means for rebar-brainstorm specifically — e.g. a footer link in brainstorm's SKILL.md, or a pointer within the plan-review gate section — so the implementer has an unambiguous insertion point.
findings.finding: The Context states rebar-brainstorm 'treats testing only via the plan-review gate docs,' implying it has no direct testing integration point, while the Success Criteria and Decomposition require the shared test-design reference to be loaded by 'all three skills at the point each incorporates testing.' This cross-section tension leaves implementers without guidance on where (or whether) to insert the shared-doc reference in brainstorm.
findings.location: Context paragraph 1 vs Success Criteria bullet 1 and Decomposition (Task 43c9)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to the Decomposition task description, e.g.: '…integrate it into the three skills (for rebar-brainstorm, this means adding a reference link in its plan-review gate section since brainstorm has no standalone TDD step) + the examples README.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify explicitly in the task description for 43c9 whether the existing inline TDD prose in rebar-debug Steps 4-5 and rebar-implement Phase 4 is removed (replaced by the shared doc reference) or retained alongside it, and add an AC line that verifies the chosen disposition (e.g., `grep` confirming removal or confirming both blocks coexist under distinct headings).
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether existing inline TDD prose in rebar-debug (Steps 4-5) and rebar-implement (Phase 4) is replaced by the shared doc integration or merely supplemented. If replaced, internal cross-references within those SKILLs (e.g., step numbering, phase labels) may strand. If kept alongside the shared doc, duplicated/conflicting guidance may emerge. Neither disposition is stated, and no AC line resolves it.
findings.location: Decomposition — Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900; Context paragraph describing rebar-debug Steps 4-5 and rebar-implement Phase 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-sentence disposition statement to Task 43c9's description, e.g.: 'The shared doc supersedes the inline TDD blocks in rebar-debug Steps 4-5 and rebar-implement Phase 4; those blocks are replaced by a single load/reference instruction pointing to shared/test-design.md.' Then add an AC: `grep -c 'test-design.md' examples/claude-skills/rebar-debug/SKILL.md examples/claude-skills/rebar-implement/SKILL.md` returns ≥ 1 per file, and the old inline TDD block headers are absent from the diff.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC1) that explicitly verifies the examples README was updated to reference examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md — e.g., `grep -c 'test-design' examples/claude-skills/README.md` returns ≥ 1.
findings.finding: The decomposition names the examples README as a required integration target ('integrate it into the three skills + the examples README'), but no acceptance criterion verifies the README was actually updated — AC1 checks only that the Gerrit change covering 'the three SKILL.md integrations' is merged, omitting the README.
findings.location: Decomposition section, Task 43c9-9aa5-290e-4900; Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1: 'The Gerrit change adding examples/claude-skills/shared/test-design.md, the three SKILL.md integrations, and the examples README reference is Verified +1 and merged; post-merge: `grep -c test-design examples/claude-skills/README.md` ≥ 1.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in task e8d3 exactly where in code-review-tests.md the CI-context resolution instruction should be placed (e.g., immediately preceding the non-gating antipattern in the FLAG list, or as a co-located note within that antipattern's entry) so the decision rule and its operative instruction are structurally adjacent.
findings.finding: The non-gating test antipattern added to code-review-tests.md requires the reviewer to 'resolve the project's CI from repo context readable with its file tools (CI workflow files, the Makefile's test target)' — a CI-resolution protocol — but the plan doesn't specify where in the prompt this operative instruction sits. The current prompt's only file-tool instruction is a single sentence (line 14-15) at the top; the antipattern's guard is placed in the GUARD section, leaving the resolution protocol unanchored and potentially failing the instruction-locality requirement.
findings.location: Task e8d3 description — 'the reviewer resolves the project's CI from repo context readable with its file tools'; code-review-tests.md lines 14-15
findings.suggested_fix: Within the non-gating test antipattern entry itself, add: 'To determine the CI context: read `.github/workflows/`, the `Makefile` test target, and any `tox.ini`/`pyproject.toml` test configuration in the repo; if none are readable or determinable from the diff context, skip this antipattern entirely (fail-open).'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
